prerequisites: An existing Google Cloud Platform (GCP) project containing your Firestore database.
The GCP Project ID for that project, used for the required project_id config field.
A GCP account or role with permission to create a Service Account and generate a Service Account key in the project.

authMethods.name: Service Account JSON key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a GCP Service Account key supplied as JSON in the credentials_json field. The service account must have a role that grants access to Firestore, such as Cloud Datastore User. This field is optional in the connector configuration.

setupSteps.title: Identify your GCP project
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, select the project where your Firestore (Cloud Firestore) database lives. Note its Project ID; you will enter it in the required project_id field.

setupSteps.title: Create a Service Account
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console go to IAM & Admin and select Service Accounts. Create a Service Account and assign appropriate roles so it can access Firestore, such as Cloud Datastore User or Firebase Rules System.

setupSteps.title: Generate and download a JSON key
setupSteps.body: Open the new service account, generate a JSON key, and download it. Copy the full contents of the JSON key file into the credentials_json field of the destination configuration.

setupSteps.title: Configure the destination
setupSteps.body: Enter the Project ID in project_id and paste the service account key JSON into credentials_json, then save and test the connection.
